name: AI Coding Spec Builder (Codex)
description: Guides teams through building AGENTS.md files, Starlark .rules, directory overrides, and prompt templates for OpenAI Codex — from project context gathering through spec generation, verification, and multi-agent configuration

defaultWeights.label: Spec Precision
defaultWeights.description: How detailed and exhaustive the generated AGENTS.md should be — high produces comprehensive specs, low keeps them lean

defaultWeights.label: Token Efficiency
defaultWeights.description: How much creative latitude Codex should have — high constrains output tightly, low allows exploration

defaultWeights.label: Test Coverage
defaultWeights.description: How strictly the spec should enforce testing requirements — high mandates TDD and full coverage, low defers to developer judgment

defaultWeights.label: Scope Control
defaultWeights.description: How tightly Codex changes should be scoped — high limits to single files/functions, low allows sweeping refactors

defaultWeights.label: Code Creativity
defaultWeights.description: Balance between conventional patterns and novel approaches — high encourages creative solutions, low enforces established patterns

defaultWeights.label: Context Depth
defaultWeights.description: How much codebase context Codex should read before acting — high scans broadly, low focuses narrowly on referenced files

defaultWeights.label: Iteration Speed
defaultWeights.description: Speed vs. thoroughness tradeoff — high favors quick incremental changes, low favors careful comprehensive changes

defaultWeights.label: Human Oversight
defaultWeights.description: How many checkpoints require human approval — high adds manual review gates, low trusts Codex to self-verify

nodes.label: Project Type
nodes.description: What kind of project will Codex be working on? This determines the base template and domain-specific rules.
nodes.prompt: What type of project will Codex assist with?

nodes.label: IaC Tooling
nodes.description: Select the Infrastructure-as-Code tools your project uses. This determines which provider-specific rules, module conventions, and state-management guidelines appear in AGENTS.md.
nodes.prompt: Which IaC tools does your project use? (select all that apply)

nodes.label: Language & Framework
nodes.description: Primary language and framework — determines linting rules, import conventions, type system rules, and package manager preferences in AGENTS.md.
nodes.prompt: What is the primary language and framework?

nodes.label: Repository Structure
nodes.description: How the codebase is organized — determines whether nested AGENTS.md overrides are needed for different directories.

nodes.label: Coding Standards
nodes.description: Describe your team's coding standards — linting, formatting, naming conventions, import ordering. Codex will embed these as working agreements in AGENTS.md.
nodes.prompt: Describe your coding standards (linting rules, formatting, naming conventions) — or type 'auto' for AI-recommended defaults based on your language/framework:

nodes.label: Testing Requirements
nodes.description: Testing strategy that Codex must follow — determines verification rules and what Codex should run after making changes.
nodes.prompt: What testing approach should Codex follow when making changes?
choices: Unit tests for all new/modified code
TDD — write failing tests first, then implement

nodes.label: Codex Usage Surface
nodes.description: Where the team will primarily use Codex — IDE extension auto-includes open files, CLI needs explicit file mentions, Cloud runs async.
nodes.prompt: Where will Codex primarily be used?
choices: IDE extension (VS Code — auto-includes open files)

nodes.label: Security Constraints
nodes.description: Security level for Codex rules — determines which commands are allowed/forbidden in .rules files and what security checks AGENTS.md mandates.
nodes.prompt: What security constraints should Codex follow?
choices: Standard (OWASP basics, no secrets in code)
Strict (auth review, input validation, dependency audit)

nodes.label: Verification Approach
nodes.description: How Codex should verify its changes — Codex produces better results when it can verify its own work via tests, linting, or builds.
nodes.prompt: How should Codex verify its changes before completing a task?

nodes.label: Standard Git Workflow
nodes.description: Assumes the team uses Git with feature branches and pull requests
nodes.assumption: The team uses Git with main/develop/feature branches. All changes go through pull requests. Codex should create atomic, well-scoped commits with conventional commit messages and never push directly to main or develop.

nodes.label: CI/CD Pipeline Active
nodes.description: Assumes CI runs lint, test, and build on every PR
nodes.assumption: A CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Jenkins, etc.) runs lint, test, and build checks on every pull request. Codex should ensure its changes pass CI. Include verification commands in AGENTS.md so Codex can pre-validate locally.

nodes.label: AGENTS.md Discovery Order
nodes.description: Codex reads AGENTS.md files top-down from global to project root to subdirectories
nodes.assumption: Codex reads AGENTS.md files in discovery order: global (~/.codex/AGENTS.md) then project root AGENTS.md then nested directory AGENTS.md files. Rules merge in this order with deeper files adding specificity. AGENTS.override.md takes highest precedence for temporary overrides.

nodes.label: Spec Complexity Level
nodes.description: Choose the level of detail for the generated AGENTS.md — Lean for small projects, Standard for most teams, Comprehensive for large regulated codebases
nodes.condition: Based on the project type, repo structure, and security requirements — what level of AGENTS.md detail is appropriate?

nodes.label: Rules File Format
nodes.description: Choose between text-only AGENTS.md rules, Starlark .rules files (with allow/prompt/forbidden command controls), or both
nodes.condition: Should command-level controls use text-only AGENTS.md, Starlark .rules files with prefix_rule() for allow/prompt/forbidden decisions, or both?

nodes.label: Directory Overrides?
nodes.description: Does the project need different AGENTS.md files for different directories (e.g., frontend/ vs backend/ vs shared/)?
nodes.condition: Does the repository structure require directory-specific AGENTS.md overrides with different rules per module?

nodes.label: Prompt Template Style
nodes.description: How should reusable prompt templates be structured for the team's Codex workflows?
nodes.condition: What prompt template style fits the team's Codex usage: minimal task-focused prompts, detailed prompts with verification steps, or full multi-step workflow recipes?

nodes.label: Multi-Agent Setup?
nodes.description: Should different Codex agents specialize in different tasks (code, tests, docs, reviews) or use a single agent for everything?
nodes.condition: Should the project use a single general-purpose Codex agent or configure specialized multi-agent roles?

nodes.label: Generate Lean AGENTS.md
nodes.description: One-shot generation of a minimal but effective AGENTS.md for small projects or rapid prototyping
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an expert in configuring OpenAI Codex for development teams. Generate practical, concise AGENTS.md files that maximize Codex effectiveness with minimal token overhead.

nodes.label: Codebase Audit for Spec
nodes.description: Comprehensive codebase analysis before generating a detailed AGENTS.md
nodes.template:
Perform a comprehensive codebase audit to inform AGENTS.md generation.

Project: {{projectType}} in {{languageFramework}}
IaC Tools: {{iacTools}}
Repo Structure: {{repoStructure}}
Team Standards: {{codingStandards}}

Analyze:
1. Detected Patterns
2. Implicit Conventions
3. Testing Patterns
4. Dependency Conventions
5. Build and Deploy
6. Potential Anti-patterns
7. Security Posture

Output a structured audit report that will directly inform each section of AGENTS.md.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a senior software architect performing a codebase audit to create Codex AGENTS.md configuration. Be specific — cite file paths and concrete examples.

nodes.label: Generate Working Agreements
nodes.description: Generate the core Working Agreements section of AGENTS.md — the fundamental behavior rules Codex must follow
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an expert in writing AGENTS.md files for OpenAI Codex. Working agreements define the behavior contract between developer and Codex. Every rule must be unambiguous, actionable, and verifiable.

nodes.label: Generate Repo Expectations
nodes.description: Generate the Repository Expectations section — file structure, module boundaries, import conventions
nodes.template:
Generate the Repository Expectations section of AGENTS.md.

Project: {{projectType}} in {{languageFramework}}
IaC Tools: {{iacTools}}
Repo Structure: {{repoStructure}}

Cover:
1. Directory Structure
2. Module Boundaries
3. Import Conventions
4. Naming Conventions
5. New File Checklist
6. Forbidden Patterns

Format as ## Repository Expectations.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a senior developer writing repository structure rules for Codex. Be precise about file paths and patterns.

nodes.label: Generate Coding Rules
nodes.description: Generate language-specific coding rules — patterns to follow, anti-patterns to avoid
nodes.template:
Generate the Coding Rules section of AGENTS.md for {{languageFramework}}.

Project Type: {{projectType}}
Coding Standards: {{codingStandards}}
Security Level: {{securityLevel}}

Cover:
1. Type Safety
2. Error Handling
3. Async Patterns
4. State Management
5. Performance
6. Framework Conventions
7. Forbidden Patterns

Format as ## Coding Rules.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an expert developer writing coding rules for an AI coding agent. Rules must be specific to the language and framework.

nodes.label: Generate Testing Rules
nodes.description: Generate testing requirements — what to test, how to test, coverage expectations
nodes.template:
Generate the Testing Requirements section of AGENTS.md.

Language: {{languageFramework}}
Testing Approach: {{testingApproach}}
Verification: {{verificationMode}}
CI Active: {{ciExists}}

Cover:
1. Test Naming
2. Coverage Rules
3. Test Structure
4. Mocking Strategy
5. Test Data
6. Verification Commands
7. CI Integration

Format as ## Testing Requirements with runnable commands.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a test engineering expert writing testing rules for an AI coding agent. Include exact commands to run.

nodes.label: Generate Security Rules
nodes.description: Generate security constraints — secrets handling, auth patterns, input validation
nodes.template:
Generate the Security Constraints section of AGENTS.md.

Security Level: {{securityLevel}}
Language: {{languageFramework}}
Project Type: {{projectType}}

Cover:
1. Secrets Management
2. Authentication
3. Input Validation
4. Dependency Security
5. Access Control
6. Forbidden Actions
7. Logging

Format as ## Security Constraints.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a security engineer writing security rules for an AI coding agent. Rules must be concrete and enforceable.

nodes.label: Generate Documentation Rules
nodes.description: Generate documentation standards — what to document, JSDoc/docstring conventions
nodes.template:
Generate the Documentation Standards section of AGENTS.md.

Language: {{languageFramework}}
Project Type: {{projectType}}

Cover:
1. Inline Documentation
2. README Updates
3. API Documentation
4. Architecture Decisions
5. Changelog
6. Code Comments

Format as ## Documentation Standards.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a technical writer creating documentation rules for an AI coding agent. Focus on rules that produce useful documentation without busywork.

nodes.label: Generate Starlark Rules
nodes.description: Generate a .rules file using Starlark syntax with prefix_rule() for allow/prompt/forbidden command decisions
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an expert in configuring Codex Starlark rules files. Generate well-organized .rules files that balance developer velocity with safety.

nodes.label: Generate Frontend Override
nodes.description: Generate a frontend/AGENTS.md with UI-specific rules that override or extend the root AGENTS.md
nodes.template:
Generate a frontend/AGENTS.md directory override for Codex.

Language: {{languageFramework}}
Project Type: {{projectType}}

Add frontend-specific rules for:
1. Component Conventions
2. Styling Rules
3. Accessibility
4. Performance
5. Testing Overrides
6. Build and Dev
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a frontend architect writing Codex directory overrides. Do not repeat root rules — only add new ones or override behavior.

nodes.label: Generate Backend Override
nodes.description: Generate a backend/AGENTS.md with API/service-specific rules
nodes.template:
Generate a backend/AGENTS.md directory override for Codex.

Language: {{languageFramework}}
Project Type: {{projectType}}
Security Level: {{securityLevel}}

Add backend-specific rules for:
1. API Design
2. Database
3. Authentication
4. Error Responses
5. Performance
6. Testing Overrides
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a backend architect writing Codex directory overrides. Focus on patterns unique to server-side code.

nodes.label: Generate Prompt Templates
nodes.description: Generate reusable prompt templates for common Codex workflows — feature implementation, bug fixing, refactoring
nodes.template:
Generate reusable Codex prompt templates for common workflows.

Project: {{projectType}} in {{languageFramework}}
Codex Surface: {{codexSurface}}
Testing: {{testingApproach}}
Verification: {{verificationMode}}

Generate templates for:
1. Feature Implementation
2. Bug Fix
3. Refactoring
4. Test Writing
5. Code Review
6. Documentation Update
7. Dependency Update

Each with clear task description, explicit constraints, verification steps, and context hints.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a prompt engineering expert writing Codex workflow templates. Each template should be copy-paste ready with placeholders.

nodes.label: Generate Multi-Agent Config
nodes.description: Configure specialized Codex agents with different roles — code, tests, docs, reviews, security
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an expert in configuring multi-agent AI coding workflows. Each agent should have clearly defined boundaries.

nodes.label: Compile Complete AGENTS.md
nodes.description: Assemble all generated sections into a complete, production-ready AGENTS.md file
nodes.systemPrompt: You are assembling the final AGENTS.md file. It must be internally consistent, practical, and complete. Ensure every rule is testable.

nodes.label: Generate Global AGENTS.md
nodes.description: Generate a cross-project global ~/.codex/AGENTS.md with universal rules
nodes.systemPrompt: You are writing a global Codex configuration at ~/.codex/AGENTS.md loaded for EVERY project. Keep rules truly universal.

nodes.label: Generate Override Template
nodes.description: Generate an AGENTS.override.md template for temporary rule overrides during sprints, experiments, or hotfixes
nodes.template:
Generate an AGENTS.override.md template for temporary Codex rule overrides.

Project: {{projectType}} in {{languageFramework}}

Generate template with:
1. Override Header (expiration date, reason, approver)
2. Sprint-Specific Overrides
3. Hotfix Overrides
4. Experiment Overrides
5. Cleanup Reminder
nodes.systemPrompt: You are writing an AGENTS.override.md template. This file temporarily overrides AGENTS.md rules. Mark as temporary with expiration date.

nodes.label: Generate Verification Steps
nodes.description: Generate the verification configuration — exact commands and checks Codex should run
nodes.template:
Generate the Verification Configuration for AGENTS.md.

Language: {{languageFramework}}
Verification Mode: {{verificationMode}}
Testing: {{testingApproach}}
CI Active: {{ciExists}}

Generate checklist with exact commands:
1. Pre-Change Checks
2. Post-Change Lint
3. Type Checking
4. Test Execution
5. Build Verification
6. Security Scan
7. Completion Criteria

Format as ## Verification Checklist.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a DevOps engineer writing verification steps for an AI coding agent. Every step must have an exact, runnable command.

nodes.label: Review & Iterate Spec
nodes.description: Review the generated spec for completeness, consistency, and effectiveness
nodes.template:
Review the generated AGENTS.md specification.

Project: {{projectType}} in {{languageFramework}}

Quality checks:
1. Consistency Audit
2. Completeness Check
3. Actionability Test
4. Token Efficiency
5. Priority Conflicts
6. Discovery Order
7. Improvement Suggestions

Output structured review report.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a senior engineering lead reviewing an AGENTS.md spec. Be critical but constructive. Focus on rules that would cause Codex to make mistakes.

nodes.label: Export Final Spec Suite
nodes.description: Export the complete specification suite — AGENTS.md, .rules, directory overrides, global config, and override template
nodes.template:
Generate the final exported specification suite for Codex.

Project: {{projectType}} in {{languageFramework}}
IaC Tools: {{iacTools}}

Produce:
1. AGENTS.md (project root)
2. .codex/rules (if Starlark selected)
3. frontend/AGENTS.md (if overrides selected)
4. backend/AGENTS.md (if overrides selected)
5. ~/.codex/AGENTS.md (global config)
6. AGENTS.override.md (template)

Include Implementation Guide with file placement, validation, iteration, and team onboarding steps.
nodes.systemPrompt: You are producing the final deliverable — a complete Codex specification suite. Every file must be production-ready and copy-paste-ready.
